questions.question: Define the entropy H(X) of a discrete random variable X taking values {x1,…,xn} with probabilities p(xi). Provide the formula and compute the entropy for a biased coin that lands heads with probability p (0 ≤ p ≤ 1).
keyPoints: Entropy formula: H(X) = -∑_{i} p(xi) log2 p(xi)
Binary entropy: H(p) = -p log2 p - (1-p) log2(1-p)
For p=0 or 1, entropy is 0 (no uncertainty)
Interpret entropy as measure of uncertainty in bits

questions.question: Define mutual information I(X;Y) between two discrete random variables X and Y. Express I(X;Y) in terms of entropies and give one example of how it measures the reduction in uncertainty about X given knowledge of Y.
keyPoints: I(X;Y) = H(X) - H(X|Y) = H(Y) - H(Y|X) = H(X) + H(Y) - H(X,Y)
Mutual information is non‑negative and symmetric
I(X;Y) = 0 iff X and Y are independent
Example: For a perfectly correlated pair (X=Y), I(X;Y)=H(X)=H(Y)

questions.question: Consider two binary random variables X and Y with the following joint probability distribution: P(X=0,Y=0)=0.35, P(X=0,Y=1)=0.15, P(X=1,Y=0)=0.10, P(X=1,Y=1)=0.40. (a) Compute the marginal entropies H(X) and H(Y). (b) Find the joint entropy H(X,Y). (c) Determine the conditional entropies H(X|Y) and H(Y|X). (d) Calculate the mutual information I(X;Y) and verify that I(X;Y)=H(X)-H(X|Y). (e) Let P = [0.5,0.5] and Q = [0.75,0.25] be two distributions on {0,1}. Compute the Kullback‑Leibler divergence D(P‖Q) and D(Q‖P). Discuss why D(P‖Q) ≠ D(Q‖P) in general.
keyPoints: Marginal probabilities: P(X=0)=0.5, P(X=1)=0.5; P(Y=0)=0.45, P(Y=1)=0.55
Joint entropy: H(X,Y) = -∑_{x,y} P(x,y) log2 P(x,y) ≈ 1.846 bits
Conditional entropies: H(X|Y) = H(X,Y) - H(Y) ≈ 0.842 bits; similarly H(Y|X)
Mutual information: I(X;Y) = H(X) - H(X|Y) ≈ 0.158 bits; KL divergences: D(P‖Q)=0.188 bits, D(Q‖P)=0.138 bits

questions.question: (a) Define the Kullback‑Leibler (KL) divergence D(P‖Q) between two discrete probability distributions P and Q on the same alphabet. State two fundamental properties of KL divergence. (b) Show that mutual information can be expressed as I(X;Y) = D(P_{X,Y} ‖ P_X × P_Y), where P_{X,Y} is the joint distribution and P_X × P_Y is the product of the marginals. (c) Use the non‑negativity of KL divergence to prove that I(X;Y) ≥ 0, with equality iff X and Y are independent. (d) Provide a small numerical example (with two symbols) illustrating the calculation of D(P‖Q) and verifying the inequality D(P‖Q) ≥ 0.
keyPoints: KL divergence definition: D(P‖Q)=∑_i P(i) log2(P(i)/Q(i)), assumes Q(i)=0 ⇒ P(i)=0
Properties: D(P‖Q) ≥ 0 (information inequality) and D(P‖Q) ≠ D(Q‖P) (asymmetry)
Express I(X;Y) as KL between joint and product of marginals, then non‑negativity yields I≥0
Example: P=[0.5,0.5], Q=[0.8,0.2] gives D(P‖Q)=0.322 bits, D(Q‖P)=0.237 bits, both non‑negative
